defs: A facsimile plate made by electrotypy for use in printing; also, an impression or print from such plate. Also used adjectively.
notes: ☞ The face of an electrotype consists of a shell of copper, silver, or the like, produced by the action of an electrical current upon a plate of metal and a wax mold suspended in an acid bath and connected with opposite poles of the battery. It is backed up with a solid filling of type metal.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Electrotyped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Electrotyping (?).]

defs: To make facsimile plates of by the electrotype process; as, to electrotype a page of type, a book, etc. See Electrotype, n.
